LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS KATO, FEDER & SUZUKI, LLP AND MELANIE EMMONS' DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANTS MAKE FOUR ARGUMENTS WHY THEY BELIEVE THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION FAIL TO STATE COGNIZABLE CLAIMS. ON THIS DEMURRER, ALL FOUR ARGUMENTS LACK MERIT. ***SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING*** COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference

TED C. LINDQUIST, III, Esq., LINDQUISTLAW 415-722-3688 tlindquist@lindquistlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff DAVID MANDELBROT, individually and as successor Trustee of the AVINOAM MANDELBROT FAMILY TRUST dated July 19, 2005

KRISTIN C. CHOI, ESQ., LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP 415.362.2580 Kristin.Choi@lewisbrisbois.com Attorney for Defendants KATO, FEDER & SUZUKI, LLP and MELANIE J. EMMONS

Sarah Arellano, CSR# 14053, 408-802-7947sarah.k.satrllsno@gmail.com Court reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS MELANIE EMMONS, AN INDIVIDUAL, and KATO, FEDER & SUZUKI, LLP's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendants Kato, Feder & Suzuki, LLP and Melanie Emmons' motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint is denied as to lines 10-13 on page 15 (damages for trustee fees and estate management fees and trust administration attorneys' fees and costs) and granted as to paragraphs 65 and 73 (successor trustee authority and standing).

While Judge Van Aken's January 22, 2026 order did not explicitly provide for additional allegations of damages, nothing in that order suggests that, had the issue been raised with her, she would have prohibited it. Judicial economy strongly favors allowing the plaintiff to allege these additional items of damages without need for a motion to amend absent some substantive deficiency. Defendants' contentions that the additional items of damages are not recoverable is not amenable on a demurrer. Because the additional items of damages are properly alleged, recoverability depends on whether the plaintiff is able to prove entitlement to those items, which is not an issue amenable for resolution on a motion to strike. Judge Moody's rulings do not preclude the plaintiff from recovering these items of damages from defendants.

Paragraphs 65 and 73 are stricken because those paragraphs contravene the explicit ruling in Judge Van Aken's January 22, 2026 order that in his capacity as successor trustee plaintiff may not pursue his first and second claims.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Sarah Arellano, CSR# 14053, 408-802-7947sarah.k.satrllsno@gmail.com Reported (301/HEK)

Sarah Arellano, CSR# 14053, 408-802-7947 sarah.k.satrllsno@gmail.com Court reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS MELANIE EMMONS, AN INDIVIDUAL, and KATO, FEDER & SUZUKI, LLP'SEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendants Kato, Feder & Suzuki, LLP and Melanie Emmons' demurrer to the first and second causes of action for professional negligence and breach of fiduciary duty in the first amended complaint is overruled as to both causes of action.

Defendants make four arguments why they believe the first and second causes of action fail to state cognizable claims. On this demurrer, all four arguments lack merit.

***SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING***

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.
